serving.natural_language_portion: A standard 100g raw skinless duck breast portion, equivalent to roughly one small breast fillet before cooking, yielding approximately 70g cooked meat.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible carbohydrate content confirmed by USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171470
gi_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
gi_source.source_match_notes: Raw skinless duck breast contains 0g carbohydrate per 100g as confirmed by USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171470. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. No glycaemic response is expected from consumption of this food. Per the Project Veritas v2 critical rule for low/zero-carbohydrate foods, glycemic_index is set to null and gi_category to not_applicable. The carbohydrate content of 0g/100g is directly confirmed by the approved USDA SR Legacy source; no GI testing is required or applicable.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (76mg/100g raw) and MUFA-dominant fat profile
hypertension.guidance: Raw skinless duck breast provides only 76mg sodium per 100g and a MUFA-dominant fat profile (oleic acid ~3.5g/100g, saturated fat only 1.8g/100g), making it a heart-friendly protein choice for hypertensive individuals when prepared without added salt or high-sodium sauces.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate, high protein (19g/100g), moderate MUFA fat
type2_diabetes.guidance: With zero carbohydrate and a glycemic index that is not applicable, raw skinless duck breast causes no glycaemic response and provides 19g high-quality protein per 100g, making it an excellent protein source for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: MUFA-dominant fat profile with low saturated fat (1.8g/100g) and high potassium (271mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The MUFA-dominant fat profile of skinless duck breast (oleic acid ~3.5g/100g, saturated fat only 1.8g/100g) is cardiovascularly comparable to olive oil in fat quality, and the high potassium content (271mg/100g) supports vascular health, making it a suitable lean protein for cardiovascular disease management when skin is removed.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High iron (2.8mg/100g) and zinc (2.7mg/100g) with MUFA-dominant, low saturated fat profile
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Duck breast provides 2.8mg iron per 100g raw — approximately 4× more than chicken breast — making it the best poultry iron source for iron-deficient rheumatoid arthritis patients who wish to avoid red meat, while its MUFA-dominant fat profile avoids pro-inflammatory saturated fat loading.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Duck Breast (Raw, Skinless) is the uncooked breast meat of domestic duck (Anas platyrhynchos domesticus) with skin and visible fat removed. Per 100g raw: 140 kcal, 19g protein, 6.6g total fat (MUFA-dominant: oleic acid ~3.5g, saturated fat only 1.8g, polyunsaturated ~0.9g), 0g carbohydrate, 76mg sodium, 271mg potassium, 2.8mg iron, 2.7mg zinc, 13.6mcg selenium, 203mg phosphorus, 22mg magnesium, 0.46mg riboflavin, 5.9mg niacin, 0.34mg vitamin B6, 0.4mcg vitamin B12. Iron content is approximately 4× higher than chicken breast, making duck the best poultry iron source. The MUFA-dominant fat profile is cardiovascularly comparable to olive oil in fat quality. Glycemic index is not applicable (zero carbohydrate). Cooking yield: 100g raw skinless yields approximately 70g cooked. Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171470. Confidence: high. Note: restaurant duck breast is almost universally served skin-on (~200 kcal, ~14g fat per 100g raw); this record covers the skinless raw reference for home cooking tracking. Suitable for halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free diets.
common_user_queries: duck breast vs chicken breast nutrition
is duck breast good for diabetes

Note: restaurant duck breast is almost universally served skin-on (~200 kcal, ~14g fat per 100g raw); this record covers the skinless raw reference for home cooking tracking.
